message:
feat(api,dispatch): unify value_and_grad into a single shared forward pass

Previously, ValueAndGradWrapped::call() issued TWO separate dispatch
calls: one with Transform::Jit to compute the function value, and a
second with Transform::Grad to compute the gradient. This was both
wasteful (the forward pass runs twice) and semantically incorrect for
programs where side effects or stochastic ops exist, since the two
evaluations could diverge.

This commit replaces the dual-dispatch approach with a single dispatch
that carries a "value_and_grad" compile option through to the dispatch
layer, where execute_grad() now branches on wants_value_and_grad() to
call fj_ad::value_and_grad_jaxpr() — the AD engine's native combined
forward-and-backward routine — returning [values..., gradient] in one
output vector. The API layer then splits the flat output vector back
into (values, gradients) based on the jaxpr's outvar count.

Detailed changes:

  fj-api/src/transforms.rs:
  - Introduce dispatch_with_options() that accepts a BTreeMap of
    compile_options, generalizing dispatch_with() which now delegates
    to it with an empty map. This avoids duplicating the DispatchRequest
    construction logic for callers that need custom options.
  - Rewrite ValueAndGradWrapped::call() to build a single dispatch with
    compile_options containing {"value_and_grad": "true"} and
    Transform::Grad, then split the returned outputs into (values,
    gradients) by consulting self.jaxpr.outvars.len(). Includes a
    bounds check that returns ApiError::EvalError if the dispatch
    returns fewer outputs than expected (value_len + 1).

  fj-dispatch/src/lib.rs:
  - Add wants_value_and_grad() helper that parses the compile option
    with case-insensitive matching for "true", "1", or "yes".
  - Thread compile_options through to execute_grad() (previously it
    only received root_jaxpr, tail, args, backend, device).
  - In execute_grad(), before the default grad-only path, check
    wants_value_and_grad(compile_options) and if set, call
    fj_ad::value_and_grad_jaxpr() to get both function outputs and
    input gradients from a single forward+backward pass. The first
    gradient is appended to the values vector to match the API
    contract of [values..., grad_of_first_input].
  - Add dispatch_value_and_grad_mode_square_scalar test verifying
    f(x)=x^2 at x=3 returns [9.0, 6.0] (value and derivative).
  - Add dispatch_value_and_grad_mode_changes_cache_key test ensuring
    the compile_options difference causes distinct cache keys, so
    plain grad and value_and_grad never collide in caching.

  fj-api/src/lib.rs:
  - Add assertions in the value_and_grad_square test to verify that
    value.len() == 1 and gradient.len() == 1, catching regressions
    where the output splitting logic returns wrong cardinality.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
